description: Vectorized NumPy array access for rigid block attributes.

functions.name: damp
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the rblock local damping.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.rblockarray.damp()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

functions.name: density
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the rblock density.

examples.code:
import itasca as it

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

examples.code:

functions.name: euler
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the rblock orientation.

examples.code:
import itasca as it

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
This function is version-specific within the supported PFC 6.0/7.0/9.0 documentation set.

functions.name: extra
functions.signature: itasca.rblockarray.extra (slot : int) -> array float{rblock} or float{rblock,3}.
functions.description: Get the rblock extra data in the given slot as an array. Extra variables accessed by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.rblockarray.extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: fill_damp
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the rblock local damping. The array must be the correct shape.

examples.code:
import numpy as np
import itasca as it
data = np.empty_like(it.rblockarray.damp())
it.rblockarray.fill_damp(data)
examples.description: Fill a preallocated NumPy array to avoid allocating a new result array.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
The destination array must already have the correct shape and dtype.

functions.name: set_damp
functions.description: Set the rblock local damping from an array.

examples.code:

values = it.rblockarray.set_damp()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_extra
functions.signature: itasca.rblockarray.set_extra (slot: int, data: array float{rblock} or float{rblock, 3}) -> None.
functions.description: Set the rblock extra data in the given slot with an array. Extra variables set by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.rblockarray.set_extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: set_group
functions.signature: itasca.rblockarray.set_group (membership: array bool{rblock}, group_name: str, slot=1) -> None.
functions.description: Set rblock group from an array. Where membership True set the corresponding rblock to be a member of group group_name in the given slot.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.rblockarray.set_group()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_moi_prin_real
functions.description: Set the rblock principal moments of inertia from an array. When modified no other rblock attributes are changed (e.g., pebble sizes, rblock volume, etc.). The specification of the moment of inertia in this way results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a rblock unless the user changes the fix state.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.rblockarray.set_moi_prin_real()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_pos
functions.description: Set the rblock centroid location from an array.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
positions = it.rblockarray.pos()
positions[:, 2] += 0.1
it.rblockarray.set_pos(positions)
examples.description: Modify positions in bulk and write them back.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_vol
functions.description: Set the rblock volume from an array. In 2D this is the volume per unit thickness. When modified no other rblock attributes are changed (e.g., moment of inertia, pebble sizes, etc.). The specification of the volume in this way results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a rblock unless the user changes the fix state.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.rblockarray.set_vol()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
